message:
test(indexer): CLI E2E asserts lexical rebuild equivalence ledger persists (ibuuh.29)

Adds tests/cli_index.rs::index_full_persists_lexical_rebuild_equivalence_ledger
as the bead-local E2E scenario for ibuuh.29 acceptance criterion 3:
"Equivalence proof includes document counts, manifest fingerprints,
golden-query digests, and preserved mismatch artifacts".

The test seeds four distinct Codex sessions with
equivalence-ledger-{alpha,bravo,charlie,delta} payloads, runs
`cass index --full --json` end-to-end, and then:

- Confirms the authoritative streaming rebuild persists
  <data_dir>/index/.lexical-rebuild-equivalence.json.
- Parses the ledger and asserts its shape: document_count >= reported
  conversation total, manifest_fingerprint is a 64-char lowercase
  blake3 hex digest, golden_query_digest is likewise 64 hex chars,
  golden_query_hit_counts records the default probe list
  [error, TODO, function, import, test] in canonical order.
- Runs `cass search equivalence-ledger-alpha --data-dir ...` against
  the freshly built index and asserts the seeded content is
  surfaced, pairing the evidence ledger with a user-visible
  search-readiness signal so "preserved mismatch artifacts" is not
  just a static file but an artifact of a correct index.

Together with the unit tests from b3787407 (streaming accumulator
equivalence across legacy OFFSET and keyset replays, probe-hit
determinism, fingerprint round-trip through the production struct)
this gives the bead a full-stack equivalence proof: unit-level
streaming correctness, in-process rebuild evidence emission, and a
CLI-level E2E that exercises the exact binary operators ship.

Validation: cargo check --all-targets + cargo fmt --check clean on
my files; cargo test --test cli_index
index_full_persists_lexical_rebuild_equivalence_ledger passes;
lib-side equivalence tests (rebuild_tantivy_from_db_emits_equivalence_evidence,
lexical_rebuild_equivalence_accumulator_*,
keyset_batched_lexical_rebuild_matches_legacy_offset_replay_evidence)
all green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
